item.dc:creator: AllAnusMorissette

item.title: The FINAL MythBusters Auction
item.description:
I'm realizing it's been a long time since I posted here, and I apologize for that! But I'm posting today for a good reason -- there are just five days left of the FINAL MythBusters auction, benefitting the Grant Imahara STEAM Foundation. There are a lot of blueprints in this one (all signed by me and Kari Byron), and all bidding started at $50 to keep things affordable. Check out the auction and/or bid at Prop Store. https://propstoreauction.com/auctions/catalog/id/390
Grant's robot baby from Battle of the Sexes 2. It needed some repairs first: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uZHmxsuEL8w
Kari signed all the blueprints in the auction along with me.
More about how MythBusters Blueprints were drawn, here https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cHCGCtIo_pM
Watch the video here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_b6cd63eTQE
Poor Buster. You can own the very last chunks by bidding here: https://propstoreauction.com/auctions/catalog/id/390
item.dc:creator: mistersavage

item.title: It's the unhinged XL Sunday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings, online friends
i'm sorry what
are you guys getting your manors?
that's gonna be a no from me, dawg
water is important gang
wtf my aunts aren't teaching me any cool tricks
should prob not be legal?
disaster
i knew it
let's get Larry on the case
ok
mmm hmm
yes?
i'm on to you
i'm gonna allow it
every time
that's all for now.. have a great night gang!!

item.dc:creator: ssumner13

item.title: I genuinely cannot think about the holidays without cold flashes and getting sick.
item.description: First off, I'm going to acknowledge that this is going to be a controversial statement for many people. Lots of you probably love Christmas, and that's fine. To be fairly blunt, this post isn't about you. It's about me. Something has to be about me for once, and it can at least be a single post on the Internet. All I ask is that you try to understand why I feel this way. I hate Christmas. I used to not, but I can't stand this time of year. I never feel safe. I've been Santa (well, one of them) since '87. Fair chance some of you may have met me, though I'd like to keep my identity private to most. For the most part, it was a rewarding gig. Yes, kids peed on my lap. Yes, I've been harassed by the banshees you all now call Karens for the most inconsequential things. There have been many times when the suits were absolutely suffocating in the balmy heated indoor air, though sometimes I got outdoor gigs (including some with real reindeer, I never handled them myself but the kids loved them). But all of that, all of it, was outshone by the absolute wonderment I gave to kids' faces. I would talk to them about the North Pole, how good they were, and you could just tell in their eyes they had a hope for the world that they would carry the rest of their lives. One of them even asked me if I could take care of their cousin, in a children's hospital, who had leukemia. How, genuinely how the hell, could anyone possibly look a child in the eye and tell them they'd try? But I did. But there were a lot of tear-jerkers like that, and it's not what made me hang up the suit. No, I left it because everyone is a goddamn asshole now. I've had people kick the presents from the tree or sleigh or even take them outright, even though they knew there wasn't anything in them. Just because it was in reach. Frat boys "accidentally" spilling whatever liquids they had on hand onto my suit. I've even been mugged in parking lots while still in the garb because they thought I'd be less mobile. But I chugged along for the kids and for the adults who needed a sense of magic. Well, you know what? They don't need magic. They need the fear of God. Because this next guy was something else. I remember him like a picture. Something like late 20s, 30s, Christmas sweater and a coat. Cuts in front of the line, saunters my way. I thought he was a parent, but he just gets right in my lap, not taking his eyes off me or saying a word. But I've seen this a thousand times already. I've dealt with special needs adults before, and it was just as fulfilling as the children. I put on my best St. Nick and ask him what he wants for Christmas. Without ever breaking eye contact, he leans in and whispers, his whispers repeating into screams: "Did you fuck my mom?" "Did you fuck my mom, Santa Claus? Did you fuck her? Did you fuck my mom, Santa!?" The next thing I know, his teeth are sinking deep into my neck and his fists are wildly trying to cave my skull in. The doctors said he just missed my carotid artery, otherwise Liberty Place would be dealing with a dead Santa. I've had numerous stitches, surgeries and follow-ups. None of the malls wanted a scarred-up Santa Claus, and that's fine because that was the last straw for me too. Honestly. Fuck Philly. Pretty sure I did fuck that guy's mom though. Still would.
item.dc:creator: NotACanadian

38
39

41

43 AND my ibuprofen

item.title: Anna Coleman Ladd gave them a new look
item.description: After WW1, some soldiers who survived found it hard to return to their families due to the extent of the facial deformation they had suffered. Mirrors were banned in some hospital wards as some veterans took their own lives when they saw themselves in the mirror. Anna Coleman Ladd was an American sculptor who used her artistic skills to help soldiers who were disfigured in World War I. She founded the Studio for Portrait-Masks in Paris, where she and her team created realistic facial prosthetics for the wounded men. She took casts of their faces and made masks of thin copper that matched their skin tone and hair. She painted the masks while the soldiers wore them and attached them with spectacles. She produced hundreds of masks that restored the dignity and identity of the soldiers. Shown below is the before and after the image of a soldier... #### Sorry if you find my promoting my book annoying. I just want to tell you that I wrote a true crime book, it's available on Amazon. --WRONGFUL CONVICTIONS: 20 Most Shocking True Crime Stories of Miscarriage of Justice https://a.co/d/9xPpsLV

item.title: I devote this dump to my wife so I can close tabs, pt. 22
item.description:
Drink some water and take your meds, you lunatics. Me? I'll be in ALL the lanes. You can't have any lanes. They're my lanes.
Make sure your voting registration isn't fucked somehow. Go to vote.org and poke around.
Gotta tell this one to my dad.
It me.
I have plenty of enrichment in my enclosure, thank you.
The REASON for the SEASON!
I'm simple. I like earth tones, sharp knives, and strong rope. I don't care much for gold or jewels. I do like a nice cup of tea, though. Wife's great at picking good tea. Just one of the many ways she's great.
It's a 4 hour video, but I think it's worth watching if you're subscribed to any of them. I'd JUST subscribed to IH, and I've been checked out of Illuminaughti for a while. Had to unsubscribe. I'm not saying every idea I have in my head is original, but I at least have the decency to say "I heard somewhere that..." and look it up if someone checks me on it.
You want to pet this isopod. I'm petting this isopod. We are not the same.
I've been bitten by many, MANY herps. More than enough for one lifetime, and yet... nowhere close to the number that I will have been bitten by, come time to lay me in the grave.
1: I'm easily satisfied with the very best. 2: I'm easy on the eyes 3: I'm good at math and take excellent notes.
This is how they make Marines currently. The Imperium still does it in the future because you can't improve a perfect system.
Somebody put this roach in a situation, and now it's pondering an orb. This is a crime against nature.
Give them some glorious purpose, already!
Don't mess with me. I'll shit my pants. I'll shit YOUR pants if I have to!
I'm fine with this. Somebody put this creature in this situation, ASAP.
Bzzt. Chicken's done.
They're electric trains, obvs. But not because birds are natural predators of trains, obvs.
Without downloading Tiktok, WHERE DO I GET THESE?! I'm going to keep asking until I get a real answer.
Have some nice music. Thank you for your patience.
They groove AND boogie!
Words to live by.
item.dc:creator: J4nein4ne

item.title: Secret Santa knocked it out of the damn county!!!
item.description:
Yesss......
YESSS.......
HELLZ YEEEEEEEEEEEEAAAAHHHHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!!! I've been all up in my Amazon wishlist looking to pull the trigger on a new Gundam build set, and here it is! And this is my first Lego Technic set as well! And it's one of my absolute favorite cars! AAAAAAAHHHH! Squealed like a little girl! A million thanks to you @BoiledChickenNuggets Most viral edit: Holy crap youse guys! Putting in the obligatory "never thought I'd make it to FP" note. It's a nice reminder that people are still decent. And still pedantic about saying legos instead of LEGO

item.title: I like how Joe cares about all Americans, not just his base.
item.description: Source: https://www.epa.gov/ground-water-and-drinking-water/proposed-lead-and-copper-rule-improvements Excerpt: The proposed LCRI is a major advancement in protecting children and adults from the significant, and irreversible, health effects from being exposed to lead in drinking water. These advancements are based on the science and existing practices utilized by drinking water systems. Key provisions in the proposal include: Achieving 100% Lead Pipe Replacement within 10 years. When lead service lines are present, they represent the greatest source of lead exposure in drinking water. The proposed LCRI would require the vast majority of water systems to replace lead services lines within 10 years. Locating Legacy Lead Pipes. Knowing where lead pipes are is critical to replacing them efficiently and equitably. Water systems are currently required to provide an initial inventory of their lead service lines by October 16, 2024. Under the proposed LCRI, all water systems would be required to regularly update their inventories, create a publicly available service line replacement plan, and identify the materials of all service lines of unknown material. Improving Tap Sampling. The proposed LCRI would make key changes to the protocol that water systems must use for tap sampling informed by best practices already being deployed at the local and state level, like in Michigan. Water systems would be required to collect first liter and fifth liter samples at sites with lead service lines and use the higher of the two values when determining compliance with the rule. Lowering the Lead Action Level. EPA is proposing to lower the lead action level from 15 µg/L to 10 µg/L. When a water system’s lead sampling exceeds the action level, the system would be required to inform the public and take action to reduce lead exposure while concurrently working to replace all lead pipes. For example, the system would install or adjust corrosion control treatment to reduce lead that leaches into drinking water. Strengthening Protections to Reduce Exposure. Water systems with multiple lead action level exceedances would be required to conduct additional outreach to consumers and make filters certified to reduce lead available to all consumers. The filters must be certified to reduce lead. Taken together, these provisions in the proposed LCRI would strengthen public health protections, reduce complexity, and streamline implementation. EPA anticipates finalizing the LCRI prior to October 16, 2024. More: https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/biden-announces-proposal-replace-lead-service-lines-us/story?id=105266898
item.dc:creator: NoddingNabob
